quizzes.lessonTitle: Psychological safety: the toll of this work and getting support
quizzes.title: Trauma & Crime Scene Decontamination Fundamentals — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: What is the primary purpose of a Safe Work Method Statement (SWMS) before starting a trauma or crime scene decontamination job?
options: To reduce the price charged to the client
To identify hazards, controls and safe work steps specific to the site before work begins
To replace the need for PPE
To satisfy media reporting requirements

questions.questionText: Which bloodborne pathogens present a recognised occupational risk in biohazard remediation work?
options: Hepatitis B, hepatitis C and HIV
Influenza, the common cold and chickenpox
Salmonella and E. coli only
None — blood presents no infection risk once it is dry

questions.questionText: Through which of the following can bloodborne pathogens most plausibly enter a worker’s body during decontamination work?
options: Breathing normally through a correctly fitted respirator
Contact with broken skin, mucous membranes, or a sharps injury
Standing more than ten metres from the affected area
Wearing correctly fitted gloves

questions.questionText: A technician finds an uncapped needle during a decontamination job. What is the correct immediate action?
options: Pick it up bare-handed quickly before anyone notices
Leave it exactly where found and continue working around it indefinitely
Use an approved sharps container and a puncture-resistant tool to safely retrieve and contain it, never recapping it by hand
Sweep it into general waste along with other porous material

questions.questionText: What is the correct general approach to removing (doffing) PPE after biohazard work, to avoid self-contamination?
options: Whichever order is fastest
A deliberate sequence — outer contaminated items removed first without touching their outer surface, followed promptly by hand hygiene
Respirator first, then gloves, then everything else in any order
All items pulled off together from the collar in one motion

questions.questionText: Which class of products should be used to clean and sanitise a biohazard-affected surface in Australia?
options: Any household detergent
Products registered with the APVMA (or the TGA, where applicable) for the intended biohazard use
Undiluted bleach only, regardless of the label directions
Imported products with no local registration, provided they are inexpensive

questions.questionText: Porous materials such as carpet underlay or affected timber that have absorbed blood or OPIM are generally:
options: Always safe to clean and reuse regardless of contamination
Treated as non-restorable, and bagged, contained and removed as contaminated waste
Left in place and painted over
Returned to the client for their own disposal

questions.questionText: In Australia, contaminated waste generated by trauma and crime scene decontamination work is typically managed as:
options: General household waste, with no special handling required
Clinical or related waste, subject to the relevant state or territory Environment Protection Authority (EPA) and clinical waste requirements
Ordinary recyclable material for kerbside collection
Waste with no jurisdictional oversight

questions.questionText: A technician is asked to begin decontamination shortly after a fatality has occurred at the site. What must be confirmed before any work starts?
options: That police and/or the coroner have formally released the scene
That the client has paid a deposit
That the weather forecast is favourable
That all equipment batteries are fully charged

questions.questionText: Why does this course place explicit emphasis on the psychological safety of workers who perform trauma and crime scene decontamination?
options: Because the work carries a genuine mental-health toll, and workers should know how to access support such as an Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Because psychological safety is not relevant to this type of work
Because it replaces the need for PPE
Because only supervisors are affected by this work
